zone_6b_notes: Sow directly in early spring (mid-March to April) or fall (late August to September) in Farmington. Spinach is the most cold-hardy of spring greens — survives hard freezes under row cover. Spring crop bolts fast once daytime temperatures exceed 75°F (late May); fall crop lasts into November or later. Avoid summer planting entirely — Missouri heat forces immediate bolting. Bloomsdale Long Standing and Tyee (semi-savoyed) hold up best in Missouri spring conditions.

planting.best_planting_time: Mid-March (spring) or late August to September (fall)
